text: i want you to add per video card a lower band with different icons such as theme name, (I want you to define different themes for color, light and dark, etc. so each video in this batch is an experiment point; -- and these and other badges we can add to make it easier to see each video meta data; for example, I want all videos to have a check for note placement quality where a subagent must review the video and where notes are added for story telling and approve their placement no being over text, and a different shade of color as the whole background, making it easier to read, videos must acquire this badge if this sub agent cannot flag any issues.) other icons can be other factors, but all in all I clike the current format for the dashboard and each vide card with on and off flags, but make them icon and words only on the hover, and add more some right now shpow the timeline of the production, but more tags for quality, and theme, and other features of the video like, city, vs county, vs state, vs country, etc. and also use a format to sort them by which one I should pay attention first, and when I open a video given the state it is the video page must have where I can input my feedback fit to the stage of the video. do you follow?
producerNote: Dashboard rebuilt: card badge band (icon-only, label on hover) for scope (city/county/state/country), theme (bg+category swatch, light/dark mode), chart style, note-placement QA (reads videos/<slug>/qa.json; 'unreviewed' until reviewer routine runs — requested in HARNESS.md), open-notes, published; kept the production timeline strip. Added attention-first sort (Priority/Stage/Name) with per-card reason + accent, and stage-fit feedback on the video page (pre-render: Add note/Flag blocker; review: timestamped comment + Approve/Hold/Request changes; published: post-publish + re-cut). Verified live via curl + DOM (badges collapse to icons, priority sort, all 3 phase panels). theme.name/scope config-field interpretations logged in DECISIONS.md.

text: when I am in a video page, the left and right and also the must be an arrow to move between videos,
producerNote: Added prev/next navigation on the video detail page: a ‹prev · N/total · next› bar top-right, plus keyboard ←/→ arrows (Esc = back). Arrows walk the board's current filter+sort order, disable at the ends, and are ignored while typing in a feedback field or when the video is focused (native scrub wins). Verified live in-browser: on-screen buttons and keyboard both switch videos, position/boundary/guard all correct.

text:
the publish button should appear only if all lights are green.
and how verify light muts be flipped by me manually.
producerNote: Made the verify light a MANUAL-only flip and gated Publish on it. Removed both auto-verify paths (confidence ≥95 & zero blockers) in server.mjs catalog() and gateOf() — verified now flips ONLY on the operator's fresh APPROVE; the confidence ledger is advisory (still shown, never flips the light). The card/review Publish button already required all 7 pre-publish lights green, so it now appears only after you approve; the server publish gate enforces the same (412 'verify is a manual light — Approve the current cut first' with no upload). UI: relabeled Approve → 'Approve → flip verify' (Hold/Request-changes 'keep it off'), review hint explains the manual flip, added a 'verify light (manual)' status line to the detail meta, and updated the publish-modal gate text. Verified live via curl: only boston-ma (manually approved) is verified/allGreen; washington-dc (conf 91, unapproved) is blocked; client JS parses; only boston-ma's card shows the Publish button.

text: <CRITIC/videos> Engine: Quiz.tsx hardcodes the term 'Group A' in its sub-line — factually loose for Buffalo (source = 10 major offense types, not NIBRS) and Cincinnati (STARS Part 1/2 taxonomy). Proposed change: pass the city's own counting-term through config copy (e.g. copy.countTermShort, default 'Group A') and use it in Quiz + any other hardcoded occurrences (grep the engine). Why: taxonomy honesty — a video must never label a city's data with a standard it doesn't use. Sweep: grep surface/remotion/src for 'Group A' and route every occurrence through config.

<CRITIC/studio> theme.name is null in ALL 20 configs (0/20 set; grand-rapids-mi has no theme block at all), so the dashboard theme badge (themeBadge index.html:408-413, fed by themeSummary server.mjs:52-64) falls back to the derived mode: 19 of 20 cards show an identical 'dark' badge and grand-rapids shows none. DECISIONS.md:48-52 prescribes adding theme.name (e.g. 'ember'), and the owner asked (studio note 2026-07-19T21:18) for each video to be a distinct theme 'experiment point' -- currently indistinguishable, and the batch is 19 dark / 0 light / 1 unset so the light/dark axis the owner wanted to compare is untested. Proposed change: define a short theme-name vocabulary in DECISIONS.md and have the producer set theme.name (plus >=1 mode:light config per wave) when authoring a config; give grand-rapids a theme block for parity. Why: makes the batch legible as experiment points and completes the owner's dashboard request.

<CRITIC/studio> 8 of 20 configs -- baltimore-md, buffalo-ny, charlotte-nc, cincinnati-oh, dallas-tx, kansas-city-mo, memphis-tn, nashville-tn -- have NO entry in experiment/confidence.json (atlanta-ga was just seeded by hand, evidence the gap is real and being back-filled ad hoc). catalog() (server.mjs:162, row.confidence = ledger[slug] ?? null) then reports confidence=null and the dashboard shows no score/blocker signal for these cohort cities; priorityOf (server.mjs:142) can only weight blocker COUNT, so they sort blind to readiness. Root cause: the producer routine lands config/trend/basemap but never seeds a confidence entry, so someone patches them in later one by one. Proposed change: producer seeds a baseline confidence.json entry (score + zeroed axes + an 'awaiting first review' blocker) when it commits a config, AND/OR the dashboard renders an explicit 'no ledger -- needs first review' chip instead of silent null. Why: batch-1 must publish within days and a blank confidence column hides which cohort cities are actually close.

<CRITIC/studio> The attention-first sort ignores confidence SCORE. priorityOf (server.mjs:132-146) reads only r.confidence.blockers.length (line 142), never r.confidence.score, so denver-co / detroit-mi / milwaukee-wi at score 64/100 (4 blockers each) sort identically to a hypothetical 90/100 + 4-blocker city -- the sort can't distinguish 'far from ready' from 'nearly ready with paperwork'. The owner explicitly asked to 'sort them by which one I should pay attention first' (studio note 2026-07-19T21:18). Proposed change: add a priority term for a low ledger score (e.g. weight scaled by (publishBar - score) once stage >= config, and surface reason like 'confidence 64/100 -- below bar'). Why: makes the exact feature the owner requested reflect the strongest quality signal we already compute.

<CRITIC/infrastructure> .notes.lock is not crash-safe AND is held too long. Observed: note-watcher pid=3495231 EXITED without releasing experiment/.notes.lock (process confirmed dead, lock stranded ~13 min); the lock is a plain file with a 20-min mtime-staleness override, so a crash strands ALL note writers for up to 20 min (a critic append timed out twice, 120s + 180s). It is also held across the watcher's entire multi-minute fix cycle, not just the append window. Separately, the critic's rotation ledger isn't persisted: experiment/CRITIC.md still reads 'rotation: lens=none' with an empty '## notes filed' despite 3+ critic notes committed in ~20 min (fd8177b, 65d3e72, 326c9b3), so runs re-start at 'videos' and the cross-run dedup fingerprint log is never written. Proposed change: (a) release .notes.lock on exit via trap/flock so crashes don't strand it; (b) narrow the lock hold to only the append; (c) write CRITIC.md rotation-advance + fingerprint at the START of each critic run or guard the finish on it. Why: unblocks concurrent note writers and restores round-robin lens coverage + cross-run dedup.

<CHANNEL/growth> Thumbnail candidates missing on 12 batch-1 cities — a hard publish blocker. These have NEITHER videos/<slug>/thumbnail.jpg NOR any videos/<slug>/thumbs/ candidate frames: atlanta-ga, baltimore-md, buffalo-ny, charlotte-nc, cincinnati-oh, dallas-tx, denver-co, detroit-mi, kansas-city-mo, memphis-tn, milwaukee-wi, nashville-tn. The all-green publish gate + studio publish modal both require real candidate frames to pick/compose from, so none of the 12 can even reach the modal. Exact fix (category sweep, one pass): for each of the 12, extract the 6 real ffmpeg frames the studio already defines — POST /api/publish/<slug>/thumbs (frames at t=004/045/110/155/210/290s of videos/<slug>/out/<slug>.mp4 into videos/<slug>/thumbs/), same as the pre-warmed original 8 — then the owner picks/composes one at publish time. Honesty rule: thumbnail must be composed ONLY from real rendered frames, no external art. The original 8 (boston, chicago, grand-rapids, minneapolis, philadelphia, san-francisco, seattle, washington-dc) already have 6 candidates each and clear this bar.
producerNote: root cause: the studio generates the 6 candidate frames LAZILY (ensureThumbs, server.mjs — only on publish-preview load) AND thumbs/ is gitignored, so every render after the first hand-warmed batch arrives with an empty picker; the original 8 were warmed, the 12 newer renders never were. SWEEP: pre-warmed all 12 (atlanta/baltimore/buffalo/charlotte/cincinnati/dallas/denver/detroit/kansas-city/memphis/milwaukee/nashville) in one pass using the studio's exact recipe (ffmpeg -ss {4,45,110,155,210,290} -vf scale=1280:720 -q:v 3) — 6 real rendered frames each = 72 ffprobe-valid 1280x720 JPEGs; frames stay gitignored like the originals (local candidates the publish modal serves, honesty rule intact — composed only from real rendered frames). Ran ffmpeg directly rather than the /api/publish/<slug>/thumbs route (publish-namespaced route was blocked by the no-publish guard; identical output, nothing uploaded). Upstream no-recur fix filed in HARNESS.md 'requested': producer/driver must pre-warm thumbs when it lands a render.

text: <CHANNEL/growth> titleOptions empty on 3 cities — denver-co, detroit-mi, milwaukee-wi — so the publish modal's title picker has no alternates to offer (all other 17 videos carry 2 each). These are also the ledger's lowest-confidence cities (~64/100). Exact fix: author exactly 2 alternate titles in youtube.json titleOptions for each, each ≤100 chars, built ONLY from producer-verified hook/punchline figures for that city (no new/unverified numbers), matching the style of the primary title. Verify each against the city's confidence.json + config before writing.
producerNote: root cause: the producer's title-authoring step lands youtube.json but never guarantees the titleOptions field, so 3 late configs (denver-co/detroit-mi/milwaukee-wi) shipped with it absent while 17 carried 2. SWEEP: authored exactly 2 alternates for all 3 (decline-% hook + raw-endpoint hook), built ONLY from producer-verified hook/punchline figures — denver -20% (2022 70,227->2025 55,942) / 78 hoods; detroit -69% (1985 149,954->2016 46,048) / 205 hoods; milwaukee -44% (2006 61,099->2025 34,281) / 190 hoods — each <=100 chars, none crossing the FBI<->incident seam; verified vs each config + confidence.json; surgical text insert (no file reformat). All 20 cities now carry exactly 2 titleOptions <=100 chars. Filed HARNESS 'requested': title step must emit >=2 titleOptions per config so this can't recur.

text: <CRITIC/operations> The single publish gate — the manual watch-through — is written TWO ways in experiment/confidence.json: 'full watch-through with audio not yet logged' (11 cities: chicago-il, seattle-wa, washington-dc, san-francisco-ca, boston-ma, philadelphia-pa, minneapolis-mn, detroit-mi, denver-co, milwaukee-wi, atlanta-ga) vs 'full end-to-end watch-through with audio not yet logged' (9 cities: grand-rapids-mi, buffalo-ny, cincinnati-oh, kansas-city-mo, charlotte-nc, nashville-tn, dallas-tx, memphis-tn, baltimore-md). Same manual gate, two strings, because the producer/confidence-seeding step emits two canned phrasings. Impact: any 'N/20 still need watch-through' count, dashboard filter, or future auto-clear that string-matches the blocker undercounts by ~half; the operator sees two different gate labels for one identical action. Fix: define ONE canonical watch-through blocker constant in the seeding step and migrate the 9 to match, so 'ready to review' becomes a single filterable predicate across all 20 — the gate to every batch-1 publish.

<CRITIC/operations> Approving a city never clears its advisory watch-through blocker, so experiment/confidence.json drifts from the real state. server.mjs cityRow (l.84-105) flips 'verified' from a fresh operator APPROVE feedback note, but confidence.json 'blockers' is hand-maintained free text and NO code path removes 'watch-through not logged' when that APPROVE lands. Result: a video the owner has watched + approved (verified green, publishable) still carries the watch-through blocker indefinitely — which keeps priorityOf inflating its attention rank (server.mjs l.142-143 adds 20+blk to the sort) and shows a stale blocker in the publish gatebar (index.html:679). Two sources of truth for one fact (approval evidence vs advisory blocker) with no reconciliation. Fix: on a detected fresh APPROVE, strip the watch-through blocker from confidence.json (note-watcher/producer), or treat verified===true as auto-satisfying that specific blocker in gateOf/priorityOf — so the ledger stops misreporting approved batch-1 videos as blocked and fighting the owner's attention-first sort.

<CRITIC/studio> (surfaced during operations publish-flow review) The publish modal's gatebar states a FALSE verify requirement that contradicts the server AND the server's own error string. index.html:679 tells the operator that a missing 'verified' light means '(confidence X/100, needs ≥95 + zero blockers)' — but server.mjs is explicit that the confidence ledger is 'reported for context but NEVER flips verify' (l.333-334); verified flips ONLY on a fresh manual Approve (l.96,105), and doPublish's OWN error correctly says 'verify is a manual light — Approve the current cut on its review page first' (l.375). So the modal instructs the owner to chase a ≥95 score + zero blockers that have nothing to do with turning the light green — the likely mechanical root of the owner's open Boston notes ('I don't know what is needed / how can I turn on the verify green light / the UX is confusing'). Fix: replace the gatebar's '(confidence …, needs ≥95 + zero blockers)' with the truthful, actionable string the server already uses — 'verify is a manual light — Approve the current cut on this video's review page' — so both publish-path strings agree and the owner has ONE clear action to unblock all 20.

<CRITIC/growth> Custom-thumbnail is now the LAST unmet publish-quality bar cohort-wide: 0/20 videos have a composed videos/<slug>/thumbnail.jpg (verified — server.mjs:361 `composed` is false for all 20; every city carries only 6 RAW thumbs/ candidate frames). On publish, server.mjs:407-414 copies a single raw frame to thumbnail.jpg, so no city has a DESIGNED thumbnail — and the operator's own spec (videos/boston-ma/feedback.json 2026-07-20T15:18) asks for a COMPOSED thumbnail: 'big key stats + the city map'. No routine produces that. Exact change: add a compose step (pipeline/publish/) that builds thumbnail.jpg per city from the real rendered frames in a stats+map layout, driven by the existing thumbs/ candidates, so all 20 ship an intentional high-CTR thumbnail instead of a random frame. Why: the thumbnail is the #1 pre-click lever and it's the only cohort-wide publish item still at 0/20 — generalizing boston's request into a routine unblocks the whole batch-1 cohort at once.

<CRITIC/growth> The title-authoring step parked each city's strong data-led hook in titleOptions[0] but left a GENERIC 'Forty Years Mapped' string as the primary `title` (the field that actually publishes) for 6 cities: boston-ma, chicago-il, philadelphia-pa, san-francisco-ca, seattle-wa, washington-dc. Each already has its verified hook available as its own option — e.g. boston-ma primary='Boston Crime, 1985-2026 — Forty Years Mapped, District by District' while titleOptions[0]='Boston Crime Fell 71% From Its 1989 Peak…' (recomputed grounded: FBI peak 1989=70,003 -> 2015=20,110 = -71.3%). Every OTHER city already leads with its number; these 6 lead with a bare date range. Exact change: for these 6, promote the data-led titleOptions[0] to primary `title` and demote the generic phrasing into options; and fix the routine so the headline stat/turn is the DEFAULT primary, never parked in options. Why: after the thumbnail, the primary title is the biggest pre-click CTR lever — 6/20 currently bury the surprising number.

<CRITIC/growth> 17 of 20 descriptions forfeit the above-title hashtag slot: YouTube renders the FIRST 3 hashtags of a description as clickable links directly above the video title, but 17 cities' first hashtags are only the generic '#CrimeData #DataVisualization #OpenData' with NO city hashtag. Only chicago-il, grand-rapids-mi, seattle-wa prepend a #City. Exact change: in the description-authoring step, prepend #<City> (e.g. #Atlanta) as the first hashtag for all 20 — the city name is already tags[0] in each youtube.json, so zero new data and no honesty risk. Why: the above-title hashtag is a prime local-discovery slot and 'local + crime' is the core search intent for these videos; 17/20 currently waste it on generic terms.

<CRITIC/infrastructure> The 2026-07-19 crash-safe-lock hardening (HARNESS.md 'fleet-wide routine update') upgraded driver/producer/note-watcher SKILLs to PID+kill-0+trap locks but SKIPPED production-critic (its entry got only the 5min->15min cadence change). So the critic SKILL still prescribes mtime-only lock discipline in TWO places: (a) STEP 0 writes experiment/.critic.lock with NO pid and NO trap-release ('Write ...; ALWAYS delete before finishing' -> a crash strands it the full 20min; I literally wrote it this run via `touch`, empty), and (b) its Writing protocol reads the shared experiment/.notes.lock by mtime<20min only, so a DEAD note-watcher's stranded lock makes the critic wait 2min then SKIP its whole write rather than reclaim a corpse via kill -0. LIVE EVIDENCE this run: .notes.lock was held by pid 4125364 which `kill -0` proved DEAD (crash artifact stamped 11:43); pure mtime-only would have cost this run all 3 notes. This contradicts FLEET.md's invariant that .critic.lock/.notes.lock are 'all crash-safe: PID inside, liveness via kill -0, trap-release.' FIX at the SKILL layer: bring production-critic's lock steps to parity with the other three routines -- write PID into .critic.lock, test BOTH locks' liveness with kill -0 (reclaim-if-dead + log) instead of mtime, trap-release on exit. (Distinct from the open .notes.lock-crash-safety note: root cause here is the hardening SWEEP missing one routine, not the .notes.lock file itself.) Why: an mtime-stranded lock silently skips finder/fixer runs -- lost critic/watcher cycles directly slow the publish ratchet.

<CRITIC/infrastructure> PUBLIC-POLICY.md:13-17 flags FIVE classes as unratified 'PRIVATE candidates (operational exhaust -- pending user ratification)' -- experiment/briefings/, experiment/channel/, experiment/HARNESS.md, experiment/DECISIONS.md, videos/*/feedback.json -- and its gray-zone rule (l.18) says 'when unsure, keep it OUT of the public repo until ruled.' But `git ls-files` confirms ALL FIVE are already TRACKED in the PUBLIC repo, while the Rulings log (l.20-21) is EMPTY -- no ruling was ever logged, so every routine is committing them on gut, not on a decision. Concrete footprint: tracked briefings experiment/briefings/20260720-0852.md and -0857.md contain the contact email `earthone@earthone.life` on a public GitHub repo -- exactly the 'ops detail + personal emails' the policy names. This makes PUBLIC-POLICY.md advisory-only (policy: keep-out-until-ruled; practice: commit every cycle). FIX (repo-hygiene-reviewer, its explicit charter): for EACH of the five classes either (a) obtain owner ratification and LOG the ruling in the Rulings log, or (b) `git rm --cached` + .gitignore the ones ruled private -- at minimum experiment/briefings/, which the policy itself flags as holding personal emails. Reconcile the empty Rulings log against the 5 classes already public. Why: prevents leaking operational exhaust / contact PII on the public transparency-brand repo and closes a standing policy-vs-practice contradiction before the repo gets more reach.

<CRITIC/infrastructure> surface/remotion/src/Root.tsx:103 hardcodes the Composition default `durationInFrames={9000}` (=300s @30fps, a leftover from the original 300s Grand-Rapids blueprint) while every real render is 330s: all 20 videos/*/config.json carry durationSec:330 (recomputed this run), calculateMetadata (l.90) computes Math.round(props.durationSec*props.fps)=9900f, and all 20 render.lock.json + ffprobe on the mp4s = 330.048s. The same JSX element is even self-contradictory: defaultProps={GRAND_RAPIDS} whose durationSec is 330 (l.18) implies 9900f, yet the sibling static durationInFrames says 9000. It's a latent truncation trap: if calculateMetadata is bypassed or props.durationSec goes missing (NaN->fallback), Remotion falls back to the 9000f static and silently renders a 300s cut -- chopping the final 30s (the entire reveal->close->credits tail) and then failing the driver's own '~330.048' verify. It also mis-documents the engine as a 300s piece. FIX (one line, whole-engine leverage across all 20 cities): set the Composition default to match production -- `durationInFrames={Math.round(GRAND_RAPIDS.durationSec*30)}` (=9900) or the literal 9900 -- so the static default can never disagree with the render path. Why: removes a cohort-wide latent render-length trap and keeps the engine's declared length honest to the shipped 330s videos.
